global_output_rules: Return one valid JSON object only.
Do not return markdown, notes, explanations, code fences, or text outside JSON.
Final output must use exactly these top-level keys: ICPReview, ICP_reason, personalized_first_line, company_category, ideal_customers, product_complimentary, value_proposition.
Every generated value must be a string.
First decide ICPReview using the client profile ICP rules, then fill ICP_reason.
ICP_reason must ALWAYS be filled, even when ICPReview is Non-ICP.
If ICPReview is Non-ICP, return N/A for every variable EXCEPT ICPReview and ICP_reason.
If ICPReview is ICP, generate every variable using the best available website facts.
Use only the prospect website content and CSV row context.
Do not invent clients, case studies, projects, services, results, numbers, industries, awards, partnerships, locations, or claims.
Never use the em dash character.
Use & instead of and where natural.
Do not use the prospect's first name, last name, full name, founder name, employee name, or contact name in any generated variable.
company_name inside the value_proposition refers to the PROSPECT company, which is allowed; the contact's personal name is never allowed.
Personalization must be about the company, website, service, clients, portfolio, positioning, industry, or target customers, not the individual contact.

variables.name: ICPReview
variables.purpose: Decide whether the prospect is a fit for the client's ICP.

variables.name: ICP_reason
variables.purpose: In 3 to 5 words, explain why the company is ICP or why it is Non-ICP.

variables.name: personalized_first_line
variables.purpose: First line that proves real research about the prospect. An observation, not flattery. No pitch, no question.
writing_rules: Mention ONE specific thing from their site: a product, platform, service, case study, project, metric, or differentiator.
Observation, not praise. No generic compliments, no exaggeration.
Do not pitch, do not ask a question, do not end with a question mark.
Natural and skimmable. Avoid: revolutionary, world-class, cutting-edge, groundbreaking.
good_examples: Your browser-based cyber range removes a lot of the friction that slows down security training programs.
The way you combine ADA compliance with Drupal ecommerce creates a rare advantage for public-sector organizations.
Your Kairos EDU-Site Framework clearly solves CMS editing frustrations for higher education teams.

variables.name: company_category
variables.purpose: Categorize the company using the category they would call themselves.
instructions: Use the category the company would call itself.
Examples: cybersecurity providers, B2B SaaS platforms, consulting firms, fintech platforms, healthcare technology companies, ecommerce brands, higher education institutions.
Avoid generic labels; use their actual market positioning.

variables.name: ideal_customers
variables.purpose: Identify three ideal customer types using a mix of industries and decision makers.
hard_requirements: If ICPReview is Non-ICP, return N/A.
Exactly three target types in the form: A, B, & C.
Mix industry and decision-maker language, based on their site/services.
Not a sentence. No em dash.
good_examples: E-commerce owners, SMB decision makers, & tech enterprise teams
Healthcare founders, finance leaders, & compliance-heavy operators

variables.name: product_complimentary
variables.purpose: A short specific compliment about their actual product/service/work, then ONE easy question about that same thing. Not a pitch.

must_do: Name a specific product, service, feature, project, or resource from their site.
Ask exactly one easy (yes/no-style) question tied to that thing.
Light compliment, casual, company-specific (could not be reused for a competitor).
must_not_do: No generic or discovery questions like 'looking for more leads?'.
Don't pitch your service. Don't ask two questions.

good_examples: One more thing, your CYRIN cyber range is sharp. Is that your flagship training product?
Your zero-knowledge architecture is a strong trust signal. Is that what users value most?
Your ADA-first Drupal ecommerce work is a rare combo. Are nonprofits your main market for that?

variables.name: value_proposition
variables.template: We specialize in {{revenue_function}} for {{company_category}}, by {{insightgeo_mechanism}}. And, I have seen how {{company_name}} {{personalized_observation}}. And, from our experience, {{insightgeo_solution}} can {{results_clause}}.
mechanism_variants: measuring how often you show up when buyers ask AI tools like ChatGPT & Perplexity for the best provider
testing real buyer questions across major AI assistants & showing where you're recommended or skipped
tracking how ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini & Perplexity describe & recommend you during vendor research
mapping your visibility across the AI conversations buyers have before they ever reach your site
finding the buyer topics where competitors get recommended to AI users instead of you
giving you a clear view of how AI tools represent you & where to improve
solution_variants: a clear view of how AI tools describe you
tracking your inclusion in AI vendor shortlists

what_we_do: Test real buyer questions across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini & Perplexity.
Measure how often & how accurately a company appears in AI answers.
Identify topics & comparisons where competitors get recommended instead.
Give a clear view of how AI tools represent the company.
Prioritize practical improvements that strengthen AI visibility & consideration.
we_never_pitch: a dashboard that gives data without direction
results_we_create: higher recommendation frequency across AI platforms
stronger buyer consideration before the website
challenges_we_solve: competitors recommended more often by AI tools
weak visibility across important buyer topics
no insight into how buyers discover them through AI

revenue_function.purpose: The growth outcome InsightGEO creates for THIS company, in the prospect's own world.
revenue_function.rule: Lead with the prospect's world, e.g. 'increasing AI search visibility', 'improving recommendation frequency', 'strengthening buyer consideration', 'improving inclusion in AI vendor shortlists'. It is the business outcome, not the method.

company_category.purpose: The category the company would call itself (e.g. cybersecurity providers, B2B SaaS platforms, consulting firms).

insightgeo_mechanism.purpose: How InsightGEO delivers: measuring & improving how the company is found, described, and recommended inside AI research tools.
insightgeo_mechanism.rule: Always frame around AI-assisted buyer research: testing real buyer questions across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini & Perplexity, measuring visibility by topic, and surfacing where competitors get recommended instead. This is InsightGEO's real work.

personalized_observation.purpose: Prove real research: how THIS company positions itself, drawn only from their site.
personalized_observation.rule: Grounded in the website only. Never invent clients, metrics, projects, or percentages. No generic compliments.

insightgeo_solution.purpose: The specific InsightGEO lever, e.g. topic-by-topic AI visibility testing, competitor recommendation-gap analysis, a clear view of how AI tools describe you.

results_clause.purpose: 2 to 3 commercial outcomes joined naturally with a verb (increase, improve, strengthen, surface, raise, grow).
results_clause.rule: Outcomes tied to AI-assisted research only: recommendation frequency, AI visibility, shortlist inclusion, buyer consideration, competitor-gap clarity. Never promise guaranteed rankings or recommendations, instant results, or invented statistics.

example_outputs_to_model_after: We specialize in increasing AI search visibility for cybersecurity providers, by testing how often you show up when buyers ask ChatGPT & Perplexity for the best vendor. And, I have seen how Acme Security focuses on threat detection for mid-market teams. And, from our experience, topic-by-topic AI visibility testing can improve recommendation frequency, surface competitor gaps & strengthen buyer consideration.

We specialize in improving recommendation frequency for B2B SaaS platforms, by tracking how ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini & Perplexity describe & recommend you during vendor research. And, I have seen how Northwind positions its analytics suite for revenue teams. And, from our experience, a clear view of how AI tools describe you can improve shortlist inclusion & strengthen consideration before buyers reach your site.

We specialize in strengthening buyer consideration for consulting firms, by mapping your visibility across the AI conversations buyers have before they ever reach your site. And, I have seen how Harbor Advisory concentrates on digital transformation for regulated industries. And, from our experience, competitor recommendation-gap analysis can surface missed comparisons & improve how AI tools represent you.

We specialize in improving AI vendor-shortlist inclusion for fintech platforms, by finding the buyer topics where competitors get recommended instead of you. And, I have seen how Ledgerly serves payments teams at growing companies. And, from our experience, prioritized, practical improvements can raise visibility across key buyer topics & strengthen consideration.
hard_requirements: If ICPReview is Non-ICP, return N/A.
Follow the 3-sentence structure: We specialize in ... for ..., by ... . And, I have seen how ... . And, from our experience, ... can ... .
company_name refers to the PROSPECT's company, never the contact's personal name.
The personalized_observation must be grounded in the website content. Never invent clients, metrics, projects, or percentages.
The mechanism must describe InsightGEO measuring & improving how the company is found, described, and recommended inside AI research tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity).
Never position InsightGEO as an SEO agency, content marketing agency, or paid advertising company. Never promise guaranteed rankings, guaranteed recommendations, or instant results.
Do not use the em dash character.
variables.style_note: Each value proposition should read individually researched: 'We specialize in ...' changes per prospect (increasing AI search visibility / improving recommendation frequency / strengthening buyer consideration) and the observation adapts to what THAT company does. Never reuse the same wording across companies. The example company names above are illustrative only.
